item.title: How to Network at Tech Events
item.description: A simple and straightforward guide to networking effectively and not just for the sake of it.

item.title: Quantifying Your Accomplishments as a Software Engineer
item.description: It is important that you keep a track record of the impact you make in your career, and that includes accomplishments outside your 9 - 5 job. Why? The record is a bank of your impact for your next salary re-negotiation, promotion or job search serves as a reference for you to measure your career growth helps you evaluate how much value you have contributed or created inspires you to do more Developer Efficiency All the work that we do (as software engineers) falls into two main categories:

item.title: A Guide to Understanding Binary Search
item.description: Binary Search - the algorithm and its implementation in JavaScript and Golang

item.title: Single-Letter Identifiers in Loops
item.description: I recently developed a practice of using single-letter identifier names in code I write. This practice is quite contrary to my principle for writing clean code. I aim to make readers understand the code I write without thinking too hard. However, I have two points of view to support this practice. identifier is the generic term for variable names and names of constants. I use the term identifier and not variable name because identifier encompasses variable names and constant names.

item.title: Why I Write Tests and Documentation and Why You Should Too
item.description: With automated testing, you can avoid repeated manual verification and testing of your code

item.title: A Guide to Handling Cross-Currency Transfers With Flutterwave
item.description: Perform cross-currency transfers with the Flutterwave API

item.title: A Guide to Interfaces in Programming for Beginners
item.description: This aim of this article it to help beginners in programming to understand what an interface is using a USB port as an analogy

item.title: Set Custom Types for docs items in mongoose-paginate-v2
item.description: With mongoose-paginate-v2 you can now set the type of the items in 'docs' array

item.title: You Don't Need to Know It All to Contribute
item.description: Nothing great is built by only one person. We all can contribute something.

item.title: How I Validated for Specific URLs in JavaScript
item.description: In this article, you will find how to make sure that users submit the type of URLs that you expect

item.title: Dependency Injection in JavaScript
item.description: Dependency Injection helps make our applications less tightly-coupled and easily testable. How is it done in JavaScript

item.title: How to Handle MongoDB Transactions on your Local Server in Node.js
item.description: Until version 4, MongoDB lacked the ability to carry out ACID transactions. ACID transactions enable database operations (creating, reading, updating and deleting data) to be more resilient and dependable. This is necessary, especially in finance-related applications that have to do with transferring money among users. This guide is for you if you use Node.js and MongoDB. It is expected that you have installed MongoDB already and you have a running MongoDB server on your local computer.

item.title: My First Open Source Contribution to Remotion
item.description: How I made my first open source contribution to Remotion - An open source video-making program built with React

item.title: The Iterator Design Pattern in JavaScript
item.description: Describing and implementing the native iterator design pattern in JavaScript

item.title: On Becoming a Senior Engineer
item.description: How I became a senior engineer at a startup and how you can too

item.title: How does setTimeout Work with the JavaScript Engine?
item.description: A guide on how setTimeout works with JavaScript the JavaScript engine

item.title: What is a Callback Function in JavaScript
item.description: A function that is passed to another function to be executed in that function.

item.title: Write Integration Tests with NestJS, TypeORM and MongoDB
item.description: Writing integration tests in NestJS using TypeORM and MongoDB

item.title: @PickAtRandom Twitter Bot
item.description: A Twitter bot that automates random selection of tweeps for giveaways

item.title: What Is an Asynchronous Function in JavaScript?
item.description: When you look at any of the hands of a ticking clock, you will find out that it moves sequentially. It points at 1 before it points at 2, and then it points at 3 and it continues. It does not point at 4 and then 1 and then 9, in a random manner. It follows a sequence. Imagine that the hand is the JavaScript runtime and each number is a function.

item.title: An Android Note-taking App
item.description: An android note-taking application built during the #DSC20DaysOfCode, 2018

item.title: About
item.description: Hi! I’m Orim Dominic, software engineer and a technical writer and here’s my resume. A Slack conversation with a colleague appreciating my work Career My career in professional web development started in 2021 as a backend developer and I have grown over the years to become a full-stack developer with JavaScript and Golang. Aside from what I do at work, I mentor (on Exercism) contribute to open source write technical articles (view my writing portfolio) Another conversation with a colleague on Slack appreciating my work

item.title: Implement JavaScript's setInterval using setTimeout
item.description: Implement JavaScript's setInterval method using setTimeout

item.title: Why Start a Blog?
item.description: There’s power in asking why. When you ask why, you can unearth the underlying purpose or reason for anything. The answer(s) to why also clarifies purpose, giving you solid foundations for decision-making. Why have I decided to start a blog? To improve my technical communication skills. To help others solve problems easily. To help me in the future. To Improve my Technical Communication Skills I live among people, and to get my thoughts across to them, I have to communicate.

item.title: Tags
item.description: Tags.. Articles grouped by topics they are about
